notes_md:
**hô** = to shout or call out loudly so other people can hear. It’s often used when you want attention, give a signal, or lead a chant.

Used for short, loud calls rather than long conversations. Common with things like **hô to** (shout loudly), **hô tên ai đó** (call someone’s name), **hô khẩu hiệu** (chant slogans), **hô hoán** (shout for help/alarm).

It’s more “projecting your voice” than just speaking loudly.  
Close words: **la** (yell, often angry), **kêu** (call/cry out, broader meaning).
notes:
hô = to shout or call out loudly so other people can hear. It’s often used when you want attention, give a signal, or lead a chant.

Used for short, loud calls rather than long conversations. Common with things like hô to (shout loudly), hô tên ai đó (call someone’s name), hô khẩu hiệu (chant slogans), hô hoán (shout for help/alarm).

It’s more “projecting your voice” than just speaking loudly.  
Close words: la (yell, often angry), kêu (call/cry out, broader meaning).
exampleSentences: Khi thấy nguy hiểm, chị ấy lập tức hô to để mọi người biết.
Anh thường hô cổ vũ khi xem bóng đá cùng bạn bè.
Khi mất chìa khóa, em phải hô nhờ hàng xóm giúp đỡ.
exampleSentencesTranslated: When she saw danger, she immediately shout loudly to let everyone know.
He often shout encouragement when watching football with friends.
When I lost my keys, I had to shout for the neighbors to help.
